title: Encyclia allemanoides
taxonomy: Encyclia allemanoides is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This plant is a member of the subtribe Epidendridinae, which is a group of orchids that are characterized by their often epiphytic habit and showy flowers. Within the Epidendridinae subtribe, Encyclia allemanoides is classified as part of the genus Encyclia, which is a genus of orchids that are native to the Americas. The genus Encyclia is further divided into several species, including Encyclia allemanoides. The classification of Encyclia allemanoides reflects its evolutionary relationships with other orchid species.

intro: Encyclia allemanoides, also known as the Orchid of the Holy Spirit, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae), native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
